source_name: Kinbaku Today
access_notes: Article bodies, translations, interviews, and historical essays were publicly readable without login; account, cart, gallery, and announcement surfaces are out of scope.

representative_pages.note: Long-form source-critical history essay distinguishing memoir, magazine culture, translation, and archival evidence.
Journal index exposes substantial history, interview, translation, how-to, and opinion archives.

decision_reason: Exceptional English-language archive of translated, interviewed, and source-critical material on Japanese kinbaku people, publications, lineages, terminology, and contested origin stories.
recommended_crawl_scope: Inventory the same-domain journal and retain substantive History, Translations, Interviews, Articles, and text-bearing How To pages. Exclude announcements, event notices, galleries without explanatory prose, reviews that contain only commerce metadata, comments, author/category navigation, account/cart pages, and image- or video-only detail. Preserve author, publication date, translator, cited-source, uncertainty, and first-person/opinion attribution.

source_name: Nawapedia
access_notes: Public MediaWiki article namespace was readable without login; edit histories, talk pages, category shells, and media-only records should not become corpus prose.

representative_pages.note: Detailed, referenced chronology, aliases, works, and connections for Chimuo Nureki.
Referenced biography and explicit Nureki/Sugiura lineage relationships.
Index spans artists, kinbakushi, models, writers, publications, chronology, and how-to bibliographies.

decision_reason: The strongest discovered structured source for named kinbaku practitioners, aliases, chronology, publications, collaborations, and explicit lineage connections, often with print or first-party references.
recommended_crawl_scope: Crawl substantive article-namespace pages for people, organizations, publications, chronology, terminology, and technique-history records. Exclude edit/history/talk pages, user pages, category and index shells, event listings with no durable context, venue directories, navigation, and media-only entries. Keep citations, aliases, uncertainty markers, original Japanese names, and source-specific claims; do not treat wiki synthesis as primary evidence when a cited primary source is available.

source_name: Kinbaku Books
access_notes: Historical essays, bibliographic notes, and commissioned translations were publicly readable; old WordPress and current-domain duplicates require canonical deduplication.

representative_pages.note: Index of commissioned English translations of 1950s Japanese kinbaku texts by Itoh Seiu, Minomura Kou, and others.
Bibliographic and historical context for an early practical kinbaku guide.
Long-running archive containing translated interviews and documentation of publications, performers, and postwar SM culture.

decision_reason: Rare public translations and bibliographic research expose Japanese magazine, book, interview, performance, and lineage material that is otherwise difficult to access in English.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain substantive historical essays, bibliographic research, and complete public translations with translator, editor, source publication, date, and uncertainty attribution. Deduplicate current-domain and legacy WordPress copies. Exclude donation prompts, comments, navigation, tag/date archives, book entries containing only cover or ISBN metadata, and image-only scans; never infer untranslated or obscured text from images.

source_name: La quarta corda / Nawame
access_notes: The English webbook, safety guide, consent guide, and many tutorial/topic pages were public; multimedia-only steps must be marked rather than reconstructed.

representative_pages.note: Long structured guide covering conditions, respiratory/circulatory/nerve risks, response, technique, and both participant roles.
Explicit syllabus for hardpoints, suspension lines, top and bottom frictions, lockoffs, rope handling, and pulley systems.
Defines the free progressive webbook, including safety, consent, history, glossary, interviews, and practical tutorials.

decision_reason: A broad, progressive, multilingual instructional webbook with unusually complete public safety, consent, anatomy, terminology, technique, and suspension-line coverage.
recommended_crawl_scope: Inventory the English educational webbook first and add untranslated unique Italian material only when it adds facts. Include substantive safety, consent/negotiation, rope and technique, history, glossary, interview, bottoming, and suspension/upline pages. Deduplicate language translations and repeated series navigation. Exclude galleries, events, cookie text, comments, tag/date archives, and video-only technique beyond explicit surrounding prose; flag potentially medical claims for attribution and later review.

source_name: Twisted Windows Bondage Safety (formerly Remedial Ropes)
access_notes: The safety hub and individual articles are public without login and robots permit the public article routes. No reuse license is visible, so ordinary copyright applies. A second body audit found that the site's own references page warns its numbering may be out of order and some numbers may be wrong; several medical and timing claims are insufficiently supported for direct ingestion.

representative_pages.note: Practitioner synthesis of individual vulnerability, location, duration, force, positioning, and environment that requires claim-to-citation verification before use.
Community negotiation and adaptation considerations mixed with health claims that must not be treated as medical advice.
First-aid discussion contains exact timelines, treatment suggestions, and prognosis language that require quarantine rather than direct corpus capture.

decision_reason: The archive retains useful practitioner-authored operational guidance, bottom perspective, and source leads, but its mixed citation quality and unsupported health, timing, and treatment claims require a much narrower evidence-gated extraction.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create only a quotation-light, attributed factual digest under ordinary copyright. Build a claim-to-citation matrix and independently verify every cited source identity and whether it actually supports the associated statement. Preserve source-labeled community observations about negotiation, communication, bottom agency, accessibility, emergency planning, restraint removal, and migration from Remedial Ropes. Quarantine exact onset or recovery timings, the two-finger rule, capillary-refill heuristics, harness-hang claims, drug or supplement advice including ibuprofen and vitamin B12, ice or heat treatment, dosing, exercise, prognosis, and all other medical advice unless corroborated by a current authoritative source; direct care guidance must defer to qualified clinicians and emergency services. Exclude copied prose, images, events, class sales/logistics, performance galleries, comments, and unrelated kink topics; deduplicate migrated copies.

source_name: Self Suspension
access_notes: Long-form public safety, equipment, upline, body mechanics, and technique pages were readable without login; the site repeatedly requires a knowledgeable spotter and acknowledges irreducible risk.

representative_pages.note: Self-suspension-specific risk analysis covering critical lines, drops, spotters, reserves, body size, and emergency lowering.
Detailed upline management, jams, carabiner use, friction, reachability, and tie-off placement.
Harness versus upline rope, hardware, cutting tools, diameter, loading, attachment, and clothing considerations.

decision_reason: Uniquely complete public treatment of self-suspension mechanics, critical-line continuity, spotter competence, jams, rated uplines, hardware, body mechanics, and emergency planning.
recommended_crawl_scope: Inventory all public substantive safety, preparation, equipment, upline, pulley, harness/body-mechanics, emergency, nerve, transition, and self-suspension technique pages. Preserve first-person incident attribution and warnings. Exclude gallery-only pages, biography, link directories, comments, and external product availability; mark visual-only technique steps and do not present self-suspension as safely performed alone.

source_name: The Rope Bottom Guide
access_notes: The 20-page version 4.2 PDF was publicly accessible without login; it is a single bounded authored resource rather than a broad crawl.

representative_pages.note: Authored guide on bottom responsibility, rigger evaluation, negotiation, anatomy, placement, body awareness, temperature, recovery, and warning signs.

decision_reason: A compact but exceptionally dense bottom-centered safety source covering partner evaluation, body knowledge, communication, tie-specific comfort, risk, and recovery.
recommended_crawl_scope: Extract only the official public Rope Bottom Guide PDF and any official version/translation metadata. Preserve author, version, headings, cautions, checklists, and distinctions between personal variation and general guidance. Do not infer content from diagrams, reproduce photography, or ingest mirrors and book/store listings.

source_name: Helsinki Shibari
access_notes: English and Finnish articles, code, and safety practices were public; calendars and current organizer rosters are volatile and out of scope.

representative_pages.note: Detailed 2025 bottom-perspective risk guide covering falls, nerve injury, fainting, equipment/environment, neck rope, and suspensions.
Authored nerve-risk article with external references and individual-variation framing.
Operational safer-space, reporting, confidentiality, organizer accountability, and intervention practices.

decision_reason: Strong bottom-centered risk education paired with concrete community safety, consent, privacy, reporting, organizer-accountability, and sober-practice policies.
recommended_crawl_scope: Include substantive English articles plus unique Finnish material on rope risks, nerves, negotiation, consent, safer-space practice, reporting, intervention, and accessibility. Deduplicate translations. Exclude calendars, event descriptions, current staff lists, contact details, venue promotions, and repeated navigation; retain dates and authors because community policies and safety guidance can change.

source_name: Anatomie Studio London
access_notes: A substantial public education/community blog, nerve flyer, code, accessibility page, and studio history were readable; ticketing, calendar, gallery, and shop are separable.

representative_pages.note: Article index includes single-column learning, consent checklists, bottoming, pregnancy, partner evaluation, disability, photography, and performance.
Detailed consent, anti-harassment, reporting, confidentiality, and consequence policy released CC BY 4.0.
Concrete physical, mobility, sensory, seating, service-animal, quiet-space, and facility accessibility information.

decision_reason: Unusually useful public material on bottom agency, consent practice, disability, accessibility, pedagogy, performance, terminology, and the operation of an established rope space.
recommended_crawl_scope: Crawl substantive Education, Community & Culture, accessibility, code, consent/etiquette, nerve-safety, bottoming, history, and performance-planning pages. Exclude calendar, tickets, class schedules, store/catalog, testimonials, gallery-only pages, venue news, contact/location details, and generic promotional copy. Attribute pregnancy, biochemistry, and other health claims and require later safety review.

source_name: The Twisted Monk Education and Rope Care
access_notes: Public rope-care and education posts were readable amid a large Shopify catalog; crawl must use a narrow article allowlist and retain vendor attribution.

representative_pages.note: Fiber-specific washing, drying, oiling, storage, and contamination guidance.
Explicit harness-versus-critical-upline distinction, rated synthetic rope, breaking strength, dynamic loading, redundancy, and risk budget.
Named guest educator's partner-evaluation and communication framework.

decision_reason: Despite heavy commerce noise, a narrow set of first-party and named-guest articles adds durable rope-care, material, rated-upline, loading, partner-evaluation, and body-inclusion knowledge.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only substantive rope-care, fiber/material, upline/loading, safety, consent/partner selection, and accessibility/body-diversity education pages. Exclude products, collections, prices, stock, reviews, shipping, sales, customer stories, newsletters, travel posts, repetitive Shopify chrome, and unsupported marketing claims. Keep all material and strength claims attributed to the vendor or named guest author.

source_name: RopeLab Online
discovered_from: public web search for anchor and upline mechanics
access_notes: Many rigorous articles, calculators, quizzes, and test reports are public, while some material is clearly member-gated; this is professional rope rigging rather than bondage instruction and must remain attributed cross-domain evidence.

representative_pages.note: Vector explanation of load amplification when two anchors are horizontally separated.
Detailed ideal versus effective mechanical advantage with pulley efficiency and friction.
Applied mechanical-advantage and anchor-force scenarios with explicit IMA, TMA, and AMA distinctions.

decision_reason: Highest-quality discovered source for the mechanics missing from bondage sites: vectors, anchor loads, redirects, friction, mechanical advantage, pulleys, dynamic effects, equipment tests, and worked reasoning.
recommended_crawl_scope: Crawl only public/free articles, calculators, quizzes, and reports directly relevant to human suspension: forces/vectors, multi-point anchors, redirects, friction, mechanical advantage, pulleys, dynamic loading, rope abrasion, connectors, tripods, redundancy, and rescue/lowering. Do not access member content or bypass gates. Exclude events, comments, newsletters, unrelated industrial scenarios, and product availability. Preserve professional-rigging context and never convert industrial/rescue values into bondage-specific guarantees without explicit source support.

source_name: Discover Kinbaku
access_notes: Public lineage, terminology/history, bottom-somatics resources, and workshop concept pages were readable; most bookings and galleries are promotional rather than corpus material.

representative_pages.note: Practitioner-attributed terminology, historical narrative, Nureki-to-Naka/Sugiura lineage, and semenawa philosophy.
First-party account of influences from Naka Akira, Sugiura Norio, and Miho Ikeda plus links to bottom somatics.

decision_reason: First-party lineage and semenawa philosophy plus uncommon somatic resources for rope bottoms add useful practitioner perspective when kept distinct from neutral historical claims.
recommended_crawl_scope: Include substantive public pages on terminology, history, declared lineage/influences, semenawa philosophy, Elements methodology, somatics/body scans for bottoms, and explicit educational prerequisites/safety. Exclude bookings, schedules, galleries, testimonials, studio promotion, shop links, podcast embeds without transcripts, and thin workshop advertisements. Label history and philosophy as the authors' perspective and deduplicate German/English translations.

source_name: Frozen Meursault Rope Anatomy and Emergency Resources
access_notes: The paramedic educator's rope-anatomy summaries, nerve reference card, restraint-removal handout, and emergency class resources were public; the broader site covers many unrelated high-risk kink topics.

representative_pages.note: Hosts a hand nerve assessment reference card and source materials.
Defines a practical rope-anatomy curriculum centered on common nerve injuries and mitigation.
Compares restraint destruction tools and emphasizes tool testing, skin proximity, tension, size, and availability.

decision_reason: A credentialed first-party safety source offering compact, actionable rope-anatomy, nerve-assessment, restraint-removal, and emergency-response material not duplicated at the same level elsewhere.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only public rope anatomy, nerve assessment, restraint removal/cutting, and medical-emergency material directly applicable to rope bondage. Preserve author credentials, citations, disclaimers, and tool-specific limitations. Exclude waterboarding, choking/breath play, blood play, rape play, rough body play, caning, class logistics, testimonials, comments, and unrelated reference PDFs.

source_name: MI Ropes Safety Handout
access_notes: The official two-page 2023 Rope Safety PDF on the site's Squarespace asset host was public; the static home exposed no readable substantive text and other handouts cover unrelated activities.

representative_pages.note: Compact 2023 safety handout covering risk, communication, nerve/circulation warnings, box-tie risk, and a curated learning list.

decision_reason: A bounded, current, community-authored safety handout useful as a concise cross-check and discovery map, though substantially duplicative of deeper accepted safety sources.
recommended_crawl_scope: Extract only the official 2023 MI Ropes Rope Safety PDF and official version/author metadata. Exclude fire-cupping and other unrelated handouts, event/conference information, and the resource URL list as training trivia. Preserve warnings and distinguish the handout's recommendations from cross-source consensus; do not infer visual-only details.

source_name: Shibari Studio Berlin Journal
access_notes: Long public journal articles were readable without login amid tuition, booking, store, performance, and gallery surfaces; author expertise and cited evidence vary by article.

representative_pages.note: Detailed nerve-versus-circulation warning signs, risk multipliers, function checks, response protocol, and explicit medical disclaimer.
Public journal index for reviewing additional safety, consent, practice, and cultural essays.

decision_reason: The best public journal articles are dense and operationally useful, but the corpus should be narrowly selected because provenance is lighter and substantial content duplicates higher-priority safety sources.
recommended_crawl_scope: Review and allowlist only complete public journal articles containing substantive rope safety, consent, bottoming, pedagogy, or performance knowledge. Exclude tuition, bookings, services, store, event promotion, gallery, portfolio, testimonials, city SEO, and generic inspirational copy. Preserve named author and disclaimer; attribute health claims and exclude any unsupported medical or physiological generalization during extraction.

source_name: Shibari Academy
discovered_from: manual full-site safety and rights re-audit
access_notes: The sitemap lists about 143 URLs across commercial course, catalog, support and public lesson surfaces. Creator Eve describes learning through a friend, books, workshops and online videos without listing medical, rigging or formal instructional qualifications. Sample public pages say nerve injuries occur instantly, suggest waiting two hours before hospital evaluation, imply moving a knot may permit continuing, give categorical rope and suspension advice, and promote an affiliated rope shop. Terms prohibit reproduction, copying, dissemination, broadcast or other use without written consent.

representative_pages.note: Creator interview describing a personal learning path without listed medical, rigging or formal instructional credentials.
High-risk nerve-injury and continuation advice with delayed-care language.
Categorical material, diameter, suspension, cleaning and affiliated-shop claims.
Terms prohibit reproduction, copying, dissemination, broadcast or other use without express written consent.

decision_reason: The completed re-audit resolves the earlier deferral against inclusion. Restrictive rights and medically unsafe or unsupported high-consequence advice each independently fail the corpus bar; commercial specificity is not evidence.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue, enroll, sign in, mirror, copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, or derive nerve, care-seeking, knot-placement, continuation, rope-material, diameter, cleaning, suspension, history, product or safety advice. Retain host, creator-provenance limits, sitemap scale, sampled contamination markers, commercial conflict, restrictive terms and rejection rationale only. Do not create product, course, lesson-title or canonical-URL recall questions.

source_name: Shibari Safety
access_notes: A full re-audit found eight public routes and an allow-all educational robots policy, but no visible reuse license. The anonymous author explicitly states they are neither a medical professional nor a certified instructor. References lean on community sites, vendors, magazines, Wikipedia and secondary health media, while the body gives unsupported breathing typologies, numeric rope-strength and 10:1 suspension guidance, boiling and oil recipes, blanket material claims, improvised anatomy zones and monitoring tests. Public crawling permission is not republication or training permission.

representative_pages.note: Anonymous evidence-based claim, unqualified-author disclaimer, curriculum inventory and no visible reuse license.
Unsupported breathing typology, anatomy zones, monitoring tests, asphyxia timing and safety assertions.
Numeric rope-strength and suspension-factor guidance plus boiling, drying and oil-treatment recipes.
Mostly generic consent structure supported by lifestyle, community and secondary-media references rather than primary evidence.
Allows educational crawling but supplies no reuse or model-training license.

decision_reason: Complete body review resolves the earlier access uncertainty against inclusion: anonymous and unqualified authorship, weak and circular references, unsupported medical and structural numbers, treatment recipes, and heavy duplication make the site unsafe training contamination.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue, mirror, copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, or derive medical, anatomy, breathing, symptom, monitoring, rope-strength, safety-factor, suspension, material, boiling, oiling, cleaning, conditioning, emergency or progression advice. Retain the host, page inventory, author disclaimer, citation-quality problem, rights gap, specific contamination markers and rejection rationale only. Individual cited sources may be independently audited; robots permission is not reuse permission.

source_name: Shibari.ph nerve-damage card
access_notes: A public one-page Place des Cordes nerve card was accessible on the site's media host; no broader source provenance was established during this bounded review.

representative_pages.note: Anatomy and warning-sign card that also recommends ice, NSAIDs, vitamin B, and rubbing after possible nerve injury.

decision_reason: The card contains useful diagrams and warning signs but gives medication, supplement, cooling, and rubbing advice that conflicts with more cautious cited sources; visual content cannot be safely reconstructed and unique value is low.
recommended_crawl_scope: No corpus crawl. Retain only this discovery disposition and URL; do not ingest or normalize its post-injury medical recommendations.

source_name: KinbakuPedia
access_notes: Public pages were readable without login, but reviewed history prose was extremely thin, grammatically incomplete, unsourced, and presented a simplistic hojōjutsu-to-kinbaku origin claim.

representative_pages.note: Very short unsourced history entry reviewed in full.

decision_reason: Sparse, unsourced, and oversimplified material is dominated by better documented accepted history sources and would increase false certainty about contested origins.
recommended_crawl_scope: No corpus crawl; retain only the rejection record for deduplication.

source_name: ShibariNews
discovered_from: manual full-site provenance and rights re-audit
access_notes: First-party sitemaps expose roughly 1,094 post URLs, six pages and 20 category routes. The About page names no editor, author, historian or safety reviewer; sampled articles have no meaningful bylines or sources and repeatedly funnel readers to Shibari Academy. History follows a simplistic hojojutsu-to-kabuki-to-kinbaku story, while other pages include unsupported posture, massage, anti-inflammatory and cultural claims. Terms claim ownership and allow only personal noncommercial extracts; robots permit access but not reuse.

representative_pages.note: About page with generic mission but no named editor, historian, author or safety reviewer.
Sample unsourced linear history with broad symbolic and cultural claims.
Sample unsourced posture and cultural terminology article.
Terms reserve site text and restrict extracts to personal noncommercial use absent permission.
Access is allowed, but robots permission is not copyright permission.

decision_reason: Large page count does not compensate for anonymous authorship, absent sourcing, reductive history, unsupported body advice, commercial funneling and restrictive terms. Mirroring it would amplify fluent but unreliable contamination.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue, crawl at scale, mirror, copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, or derive people, history, culture, posture, anatomy, technique, safety or product facts. Retain title, host, sitemap scale, sampled contamination markers, terms restriction and rejection rationale only. Do not use its outbound course links as authority and do not create URL, article-title or canonical-path recall questions.

source_name: Petzl Professional Technical Tips
access_notes: Named setup-specific technical tips and test reports were readable on the manufacturer's public Professional site. Robots did not disallow the reviewed paths; product context and ordinary site copyright still require narrow factual extraction rather than catalog capture.

representative_pages.note: Explains two-rope hauling and lowering configurations, imperfect load sharing, system roles, and training constraints.
Shows a releasable ground-operated lowering concept and explicitly cautions that the high redirect and ground anchor must withstand the load.
Discusses tree movement systems, redirect forces, friction, and configuration limits in an arborist context.

decision_reason: A first-party professional library combines operational rescue/lowering configurations with quantified or explicitly tested redirect, friction, slack, and load-sharing behavior.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist public Professional technical tips and test reports directly relevant to anchors, force paths, redirects, hauling/lowering, rescue, rope/device interaction, and dynamic or slack-load behavior. Exclude catalog, store, dealer, marketing, and unrelated sport pages. Preserve exact device, rope, mass, geometry, test, date, and professional-training conditions; never generalize a product result or certification into a bondage guarantee.

source_name: DMM Technical Knowledge
access_notes: Long authored knowledge articles and test tables were public on the manufacturer storefront. Shopify robots excluded commerce/account mechanics but not the reviewed knowledge paths; factual capture must remove storefront chrome and keep manufacturer/test provenance.

representative_pages.note: George Smith's rescue-rigging article derives common pulley systems, anchor forces, practical friction losses, rotation, heat, and edge hazards.
Reports material- and configuration-specific sling anchor tests, including slack loading, sliding configurations, failure observations, and a result table.

decision_reason: Dense worked mechanics and manufacturer test evidence add unusually clear coverage of pulley ratios, frictional losses, anchor reactions, sling material, slack loading, and configuration-dependent failure.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only complete public Knowledge articles and test reports on pulley mechanics, anchor loads, slings, connectors, rope behavior, friction, edges, dynamic/slack loading, and rescue systems. Exclude storefront, collections, prices, stock, reviews, newsletters, and product-selection marketing. Preserve author, test setup, geometry, material, units, sample size, uncertainty, and failure context; do not convert industrial tests into human-suspension certification.

source_name: Arboricultural Association Tree-Climbing and Rigging Guides
access_notes: Official Safety Guides 1 and 3 were directly public on the Association host and robots allow the paths; current full Technical Guides are sold separately. Two indexed consultation-draft URLs returned 404 during the final audit and were removed rather than treated as current evidence.

Official Tree Climbing and Aerial Rescue checklist covers tree-condition assessment, primary and backup systems, slack/fall minimization, anchor selection and testing, component compatibility, inspection, and rescue planning.

decision_reason: Official association checklists fill the outdoor-anchor gap with tree-condition assessment, foreseeable loading, system compatibility, slack/fall control, anchor testing, inspection, and aerial rescue planning.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture only freely public official Safety Guides 1 and 3 sections relevant to human-support tree condition, anchor selection/testing, system configuration, foreseeable or peak loading, component compatibility, slack/fall minimization, lowering, inspection, and aerial rescue. Preserve guide title, date, version, checklist status, and stated training limits. Exclude paid Technical Guides, unavailable drafts, cutting/dismantling logistics except necessary mechanics, and visual-only inference; never treat a tree, species, or configuration as certified for bondage.

source_name: TreeConsult Rigging Research
access_notes: The firm's download index and author-hosted papers, presentations, and HSE-funded report were public; robots excluded CMS/beta/demo paths but not downloads. The same site contains consulting promotion and unrelated tree-statics material, so only named research documents are suitable.

representative_pages.note: Official author-hosted index identifies publications, reports, dates, authors, and research provenance.
Summarizes an HSE-sponsored investigation into rigging techniques, loads, tree response, and risk reduction.
Author presentation reports measured and modeled dynamic loads in climbing and rigging configurations.

decision_reason: Primary and author-hosted research adds measured force, dynamic-event, friction, redirect, stem-response, and safety-margin evidence that is absent from bondage-specific sources.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only named author-hosted research papers, HSE project outputs, and technical presentations on measured anchor, redirect, friction, fall/dynamic, rope, and load-path forces or risk controls. Exclude consulting promotion, software marketing, client information, unrelated wind/tree-statics research, and cutting logistics. Preserve funding, authors, date, experiment, units, apparatus, arboriculture context, and limitations; no tree or bondage certification may be inferred.

source_name: FEDEC Safety and Rigging Manual
access_notes: FEDEC's official project page and multilingual 2008 manual chapters were directly public without login. The robots endpoint returned site HTML rather than rules. Durable principles are mixed with dated European legal and equipment guidance, so extraction must be version-bounded.

representative_pages.note: Official project and language index describes the manual's risk-management, training, rigging, legal, and safety scope.
English rigging chapter emphasizes competent installation and inspection, independent systems, anchor duplication, fall prevention, and planned rescue.

decision_reason: An official circus-education manual contributes useful human-performance rigging governance, competence, independent-system, inspection, and rescue concepts, despite its age and jurisdiction-specific sections.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture only durable risk-management, competence, independent-anchor/system, inspection, communication, fall-prevention, and rescue-planning principles from the official public manual. Preserve the 2008 edition, circus context, country/legal references, and translations; exclude or quarantine jurisdiction-specific thresholds, superseded standards, and dated product/equipment assertions. Do not treat FEDEC guidance as structural or bondage certification.

source_name: Introduction to Rigging: Aerialist Essentials
access_notes: The complete author-uploaded book text was publicly readable on ResearchGate and robots allow the publication path, but the book states all rights reserved and forbids reproduction without express written permission. representative_pages.note: Author-uploaded 2014/2015 book covers rigging math, hardware, ropes and knots, lifting slings, points in wood/concrete/steel, beams/joists/trusses, frames, maintenance, and safety devices, with strong qualification warnings and an explicit no-reproduction notice.

decision_reason: The book is an excellent hardpoint and circus-rigging source, but its explicit all-rights-reserved/no-reproduction notice requires permission or a documented transformed-use policy before direct-training capture.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue for direct-training Markdown without rights clearance. If permission or a compliant transformed-fact policy is documented, limit capture to source-attributed factual synthesis of structural assessment, rigging math, load paths, wood/concrete/steel points, beams/joists/trusses, hardware, pulleys, frames, and inspection; never reproduce figures or extended prose, and retain the warning that reading does not qualify a rigger.

decision_reason: It fills the performance-rig rescue gap, but an absent permissive license and related explicit copyright restrictions prevent clean direct-training acceptance without a reuse decision.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue for direct-training Markdown without rights clearance. If cleared, extract only source-attributed factual synthesis of rescue preplanning, lowering-capable systems, failure scenarios, communication, emergency-service escalation, equipment readiness, drills, and competence limits. Exclude image-dependent apparatus procedures and do not reproduce extended text or figures.

source_name: Equity Fit to Fly Checklist

representative_pages.note: Joint performer/rigging checklist covers declared loads, venue documentation, installation and inspection competence, full load-path safety, dynamic-force expertise, clear air, winches, emergency stops, and rescue.

decision_reason: A strong operational due-diligence checklist adds the performer's perspective on venue hardpoints, documentation, competent installers, entire load paths, dynamic loads, automation, and rescue readiness.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture this checklist and stable provenance only. Retain UK, performer-flying, inspection-period, legal, and collaboration context. Extract durable questions about declared load, venue evidence, competent installation, inspection, full load path, dynamic loading, clear air, automation failsafes, and rescue; do not infer DIY installation instructions or capacity and do not treat it as bondage certification.

source_name: Healing Experiences in Japanese Rope Bondage Practice
access_notes: The complete 11-page article, DOI 10.53074/cstp.2023.46, is publicly accessible and explicitly CC BY 4.0. It is an MSc-programme phenomenological study of ten regular practitioners recruited from practice groups, acquaintances and some friends; all participants were Caucasian and the interview guide expressly prompted felt healing effects. The author states that medical therapeutic properties are unproven and that the sample cannot support generalization, yet the article repeatedly promotes healing or therapy and positively frames a participant temporarily losing consciousness during bondage suspension as a near-death transformation. Open licensing does not cure positive-selection bias, prompting, overclaim, missing adverse or dropout perspectives, or normalization of a medical emergency.

representative_pages.note: Official journal record for title, author, DOI, abstract, issue, body route and visible licensing context.

Complete 11-page body reviewed for recruitment, interview prompts, participant composition, author positionality, limitations, therapeutic overclaim, loss-of-consciousness framing and the explicit CC BY 4.0 notice.

decision_reason: The study's positive-selection design, leading prompt, author positionality and therapeutic interpretation cannot support treatment claims, while positively narrating suspension-related loss of consciousness creates unacceptable contamination risk; even a caveated digest would give these claims disproportionate training weight.
recommended_crawl_scope: Remove this work from the extraction queue. Do not copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, derive QA, reproduce participant narratives, or import any claim that Shibari heals trauma, functions as therapy, creates altered states safely, or makes loss of consciousness positive or transformative. Retain only title, author, year, DOI, 11-page extent, MSc-programme provenance, CC BY 4.0 status, ten-regular-practitioner sample, practice-group or acquaintance or friend recruitment, all-Caucasian participant pool, explicit felt-healing interview prompt, author positionality, acknowledged lack of medical proof and generalizability, positive-selection and missing-adverse-perspective limits, loss-of-consciousness safety concern, and rejection rationale as discovery metadata. Use qualified clinical evidence for syncope or loss of consciousness and stronger consent or lived-experience research that samples neutral and adverse outcomes.
rights_basis: The body explicitly uses CC BY 4.0, but permissive adaptation rights do not override severe evidence-quality and safety-contamination concerns.
